citations.description: Step 3 still shows the fully manual Homebrew update recipe with no automation note or fallback label.

findings.criterion: Update docs/releasing.md step 3 to mark Homebrew automated (manual = fallback)
findings.detail: Step 3 of docs/releasing.md (lines 310-326) still contains the original manual recipe: it instructs editing Formula/rebar.rb manually and running git push, with no mention of a scheduled self-bump, the autobump.yml workflow, or a 'manual = fallback' designation. No text matching 'automated', 'self-bump', 'autobump', 'scheduled', or 'fallback' appears in releasing.md.
findings.title: docs/releasing.md step 3 not updated to reflect automation

citations.description: "version": "0.9.0" at top level and in packages[] — not bumped to 0.9.1
Most recent changelog entry is [0.9.0]; no [0.9.1] section exists

findings.criterion: Release v0.9.1 carrying the doc change (pyproject.toml + server.json bumped to 0.9.1, CHANGELOG [0.9.1] entry)
findings.detail: pyproject.toml version is '0.9.0' (not 0.9.1), server.json version is '0.9.0' (not 0.9.1), and CHANGELOG.md contains no [0.9.1] section — the most recent entry is [0.9.0]. The v0.9.1 release described in the ticket has not landed in the codebase.
findings.title: v0.9.1 version bump and CHANGELOG not present in repository

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
